label: D1a (external, skill-advisor) and D1b (internal, smart router) are orthogonal routing dimensions at different scopes: advisor picks which skill from the global set; smart router picks which sub-documents once the skill is active. They require independent scoring protocols — D1a needs a labeled prompt corpus, D1b is deterministic from INTENT_SIGNALS/RESOURCE_MAP.

D1 must split into D1a (0.15 weight) and D1b (0.15 weight) as independent sub-scores under the 0.30 D1 composite. A blended D1 obscures root cause — advisor-side failure vs skill-router-side failure require different remediations and different owners.

Properly utilized = D1a PASS AND D1b PASS (conjunction). The 2x2 utilization matrix (properly utilized / internal router regression / advisor gap / double fail) is the right report shape — it encodes both pass/fail AND the remediation path.

D1b should be computed post-hoc from the AI's ACTUAL task text (captured from the run) rather than from a pre-run prediction. The simulator becomes a post-hoc verifier: run score_intents() on the AI's own phrasing, then compare expected resources against observed Read() traces.

Near-neighbor confusion is a critical D1a failure mode: the advisor routinely produces tie collisions between related skills (live evidence from this session's own hook). Lane C must include near-neighbor confusion scenarios measuring sibling-routing rate. For deep-improvement: deep-research, sk-code-review, deep-review.

Trigger-derived prompts create a circularity trap: they test keyword-string matching (does AI match trigger phrase to frontmatter keyword?), not real-world discovery (does AI find the skill for a task described in domain-generic language?). The benchmark's core question is domain discovery, not trigger recall.

Three fixture-authoring strategies are needed: (1) hand-authored golden scenarios as calibration baseline, (2) trigger-paraphrase synthesis gated by contamination checker, (3) LLM generation from a domain constitution (skill-agnostic problem-space description) using the Constitutional AI pattern (Bai et al. 2022). Hybrid pipeline: golden (5-8) + synthesized (10-20) + constitution-derived (5-10).

Negative scenarios are generated from each skill's 'When NOT to Use' section — a built-in source of irrelevance-detection tests mapping directly to BFCL v4's 'Irrelevance Detection' category. Every exclusion class yields 1-2 prompts that should NOT trigger the skill.
The LLM paraphraser for Strategy 2 should use a DIFFERENT model than the benchmark executor to reduce circularity risk. Contamination gate runs in strict mode (exact-match + near-match detection).

Skill-advisor scorer internals (aliases.ts, explicit.ts, fusion.ts, skill-graph.json, regression fixtures) carry hardcoded skill-name references that must change during Phase 002 rename. Advisor rebuild must happen before these files can validate.

The domain constitution for Strategy 3 should be authored by a DIFFERENT operator than the skill author to avoid re-encoding the author's mental model of what should trigger the skill. Alternatively, use a generic model with only the skill's README as input.

Rename surface map is complete: 7 buckets (canonical skill package A, commands+YAML B, agent+4 runtime mirrors C, advisor graph+metadata D, cross-skill references E, install guides+root docs F, historical specs/changelogs G) covering 80+ files across .opencode/, .claude/, .codex/, .gemini/. Advisory: no operational references to deep-agent-improvement should remain post-rename; historical archives allowed.
